Title: Create a message tracking new recipients
Track new recipients = off
Successful retrieval of unread messages with valid data
Expected Result:
1. totalCount matches the number of messages with isRead = false.
2. All messages in the items array have isRead = false.
3. Each field (id, shortMessage, createdDate, isRead, isHidden) is present and contains the correct data.
Preconditions:
1. The query works only for a logged-in user.
2. The system contains both read and unread messages with valid data (id, shortMessage, createdDate, isRead, isHidden).

Title: Filtering messages by culture (Not actual now)
Expected Result: All messages in the items array are filtered by the en-Us culture.
Preconditions:
1. The query works only for a logged-in user.
2. The system contains messages with different cultureName values.

Title: Correct representation of hidden messages
Expected Result: The isHidden field accurately represents the hidden status (true/false).
Preconditions:
1. The query works only for a logged-in user.
2. The system contains both hidden and non-hidden messages.

Title: No messages available for the given filters
Expected Result:
totalCount is 0.
The items array is empty.
Preconditions:
1. The query works only for a logged-in user.
2. The system contains no unread messages for the specified culture.

Title: Clear all push messages
Preconditions:
1. The query works only for a logged-in user.
2. The system contains at least one unread push message.

Title: Mark all push messages as read
Expected Result: All push messages are marked as read, and the response does not return any errors.
Preconditions:
1. The query works only for a logged-in user.
2. The system contains at least one unread push message.

Title: Mark all push messages as unread
Expected Result:
* response contains a successful status
* All messages previously marked as read are now displayed as unread in the user interface.
Preconditions:
* User is logged into the application.
* User has at least one push message marked as read.

Title: Mark a push message as read
Expected Result: The mutation successfully marks the specified push message as read.
Preconditions:
* User is logged into the application.
* User has at least one unread push message.

Title: Mark a push message as unread
Expected Result: The mutation successfully marks the specified push message as unread.
Preconditions:
* User is logged into the application.
* User has at least one read push message.

Title: How to setup settings
Preconditions:
Open environment.yml
Set settings from firebase

- name: string
  platform:
    config:
      PushMessages__UseFirebaseCloudMessaging: true
      PushMessages__FcmSenderOptions__Type: string
      PushMessages__FcmSenderOptions__ProjectId: string
      PushMessages__FcmSenderOptions__PrivateKeyId: string
      PushMessages__FcmSenderOptions__ClientEmail: string
      PushMessages__FcmSenderOptions__ClientId: string
      PushMessages__FcmSenderOptions__AuthUri: string
      PushMessages__FcmSenderOptions__TokenUri: string
      PushMessages__FcmSenderOptions__AuthProviderX509CertUrl: string
      PushMessages__FcmSenderOptions__ClientX509CertUrl: string
      PushMessages__FcmSenderOptions__UniverseDomain: string
      PushMessages__FcmReceiverOptions__ApiKey: string
      PushMessages__FcmReceiverOptions__AuthDomain: string
      PushMessages__FcmReceiverOptions__ProjectId: string
      PushMessages__FcmReceiverOptions__StorageBucket: string
      PushMessages__FcmReceiverOptions__MessagingSenderId: string
      PushMessages__FcmReceiverOptions__AppId: string
      PushMessages__FcmReceiverOptions__VapidKey: string
    secretConfig:
      PushMessages__FcmSenderOptions__PrivateKey: fcm-private-key
Steps:
Step Description:
Open https://console.firebase.google.com/
Click Add project
Enter project name
Click Create project
When the project is created, click Continue

Step Description:
Sender settings
Click Project settings
Click Service accounts
Click Generate new private key
In the popup window click Generate key
Open the downloaded JSON file and copy all fields to the corresponding fields in the
appSettings.json
When using the YAML file for settings, remove all \n characters from the private_key field
and put the resulting value in the Key Vault

Step Description:
Receiver settings
On the Project Overview page click Add Web app
Enter application name
Click Register app
In the Add Firebase SDK section you will find settings that should be placed in the
PushMessages.FcmReceiverOptions section in the appSettings.json
Click Continue to console
Click Project settings
Click Cloud Messaging
In the Web Push certificates section click Generate key pair
Steps (Step):
1. Open https://console.firebase.google.com/
Click Add project
Enter project name
Click Create project
When the project is created, click Continue

2. Sender settings
Click Project settings
Click Service accounts
Click Generate new private key
In the popup window click Generate key
Open the downloaded JSON file and copy all fields to the corresponding fields in the
appSettings.json
When using the YAML file for settings, remove all \n characters from the private_key field
and put the resulting value in the Key Vault

3. Receiver settings
On the Project Overview page click Add Web app
Enter application name
Click Register app
In the Add Firebase SDK section you will find settings that should be placed in the
PushMessages.FcmReceiverOptions section in the appSettings.json
Click Continue to console
Click Project settings
Click Cloud Messaging
In the Web Push certificates section click Generate key pair
